Protein names: rRNA adenine N-6-methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.184) (Erythromycin resistance protein) (Macrolide-lincosamide-streptogramin B resistance protein)
Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, rRNA adenine N(6)-methyltransferase family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. rRNA adenine N(6)-methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01026}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=adenosine(2085) in 23S rRNA + 2 S-adenosyl-L-methionine = N(6)-dimethyladenosine(2085) in 23S rRNA + 2 S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + 2 H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:42784, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:10237, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:10238, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:74411, ChEBI:CHEBI:74493; EC=2.1.1.184;
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: This protein produces a dimethylation of the adenine residue at position 2085 in 23S rRNA, resulting in reduced affinity between ribosomes and macrolide-lincosamide-streptogramin B antibiotics. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:12907737, ECO:0000269|PubMed:12946350}.
